summary: Iron-law guards in pipeline-context.tsx are intact, but 11 real defects exist across rate-limit fallback inserts, optimistic-update silent failures, missing UUID-format validation, RLS bypasses on signup_requests, presence beacon write path, missing publish_job-on-approve gate, and broken record-of-publish validation for hand-edits.

findings.title: Optimistic updateCard/moveCard rollback path silently masks RLS denials — UI says 'saved', DB never wrote, refresh reverts
findings.scenario: User edits caption/schedule/platforms on a card. UI updates immediately (optimistic). If Supabase rejects the UPDATE (RLS membership expired, role demoted to viewer, network 5xx, or a future column constraint added by a migration), the .then() callback only logs to console. No toast. No rollback. The card stays visually edited until the user refreshes — at which point all edits disappear because the DB never accepted them. This is the same class of bug as the load() iron-law violation, just on the UPDATE side.
findings.blast_radius: Every field edit through the drawer (caption, scheduledDate/Time, platforms, contentType, checklist, notes, sourceVault). User loses arbitrary amounts of work on refresh. moveCard at line 485 has full rollback + toast, deleteCard at line 736 has full rollback + toast. Only updateCard is silently failing.
findings.repro: 1) Revoke a user's workspace_members row OR set status='pending' OR demote role to a non-write role via SQL while their tab is open. 2) Have them edit a card caption. 3) UI shows the edit. Console logs the RLS denial. 4) Refresh the page. The edit is gone. No toast was ever shown.

findings.title: submitReapproval + submitKickback also silently swallow RLS errors — fix submissions appear queued but never reach DB
findings.scenario: Editor submits a fix on a Revision Needed card. UI immediately moves the card to Awaiting Approval, history shows the new revision note, the notification email fires to approvers. But if the UPDATE failed (transient 5xx, role drift, RLS), the DB still has stage='revision_needed'. Approvers get the email and click in to approve — but the card is still in revision_needed in the DB. On the next refresh by the editor, their fix is gone.
findings.blast_radius: Worse than DATA-001 because (a) revision text and reapproval flow is high-trust UX, (b) it triggers notification emails that promise reviewers the card moved when it didn't, (c) the audit log via logAudit() fires unconditionally even when the DB write failed, leaving a phantom 'revision_submitted' event with no matching stage_changed trigger.
findings.repro: Same as DATA-001 but on Revision Needed → Awaiting Approval. Notification email sent, but next refresh on the editor's side restores stage='revision_needed' and drops the revision note.
findings.fix: Same pattern as DATA-001 — snapshot previousCard before mutation, on error rollback + addToast. Also guard the notification fetch and logAudit call behind a !error check so phantom events stop firing. Same fix on both 577 and 629.

findings.title: POST /api/publish-jobs requires post.scheduled_at OR legacy columns, but moveCard only creates a job when BOTH scheduledDate AND scheduledTime are set on the card — orphan approved_scheduled posts will silently never publish
findings.scenario: Approver drags a card to Approved/Scheduled. The card has stage='approved_scheduled' in the DB (which is fine; 0026 lockdown only guards transitions INTO 'posted', not approved_scheduled). But because the client used `card?.scheduledDate && card.scheduledTime` (NOT scheduled_at) as the gate, a post that has scheduled_at set but not the legacy date/time columns (or vice versa) might skip createPublishJob. The card sits in approved_scheduled forever with no publish_jobs row. n8n never sees it. The 0026 v_publish_queue view only surfaces jobs that exist — there's no view that finds approved_scheduled posts with no job.
findings.blast_radius: Any approved post can silently become unpublishable. The 0026 cleanup at lines 152-159 actively kills jobs whose post stage is not approved_scheduled, but does NOT cover the opposite drift (approved_scheduled with no job). Iron-law spirit violation: an approved post never goes live and no one knows.
findings.repro: 1) Insert a post directly via SQL with stage='approved_scheduled', scheduled_at=now()+1h, scheduled_date=null, scheduled_time=null. 2) Reload the app — the card appears in approved_scheduled. 3) Move it back to revision_needed, then forward to approved_scheduled again. Because the local card.scheduledDate is empty after dbToCard reconstruction (line 171 uses row.scheduled_date directly), the createPublishJob branch is skipped. 4) Wait for scheduled_at to pass. n8n claim_publish_job finds nothing.
findings.fix:
Two coordinated fixes:
1) src/lib/pipeline-context.tsx line 489: change gate to also accept scheduled_at:
```ts
const hasSchedule = (card?.scheduledDate && card?.scheduledTime) || (card as any)?.scheduledAt;
if (newStage === "approved_scheduled" && hasSchedule) { await createPublishJob(cardId); }
```
2) Add a SQL view + admin alert: `CREATE VIEW v_approved_without_job AS SELECT p.* FROM posts p LEFT JOIN publish_jobs j ON j.post_id = p.id WHERE p.stage='approved_scheduled' AND j.id IS NULL;` Surface it in the Publishing Queue admin panel alongside v_publish_queue.
3) Better: server-side trigger on posts UPDATE that auto-calls create_publish_job_for_post() when stage flips to approved_scheduled (and scheduled_at is set). Eliminates the client-side gate entirely.

findings.title: isValidUuid() regex accepts non-canonical UUIDs that create_publish_job_for_post()'s strict cast may reject — guard says yes, DB says no
findings.scenario: pipeline-context.tsx, media-assets.ts, audit.ts, ai/studio/* routes (admin/backfill-media, ai/studio/cancel-job, ai/studio/generate-row, ai/studio/generate-batch, ai/studio/rows/[id], ai/jobs/[id], admin/publish-jobs/[id]/retry) all use the LOOSE regex (no version/variant nibbles). Only /api/publish-jobs uses the STRICT one. PostgREST will mostly accept any 8-4-4-4-12 hex, so this is a silent inconsistency rather than a bug today — but if a card.id ever lands as the literal '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000', the loose guard says yes and an .eq('id', cardId) call propagates the zero UUID into trigger functions that DO record it as a stage change for a non-existent post.
findings.blast_radius: Inconsistent validation surfaces — different code paths disagree on what's safe. With the zero UUID, the moveCard rollback at line 528 would .eq('id', '0000…') and return zero rows (not an error from PostgREST), so the rollback silently fails to revert; meanwhile the local UI thinks it did revert. Same regex drift exists across 9 separate file copies (DRY violation).
findings.repro: Direct: tamper with card.id in dev tools to '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' (loose regex passes). Move the card. Server-side trigger logs a stage_changed for a non-existent post id. Rollback path silently no-ops.
findings.fix: Extract isValidUuid into src/lib/utils.ts using the strict regex (`[1-5]` version nibble + `[89ab]` variant nibble), and have every other file import from there. Remove the 9 duplicate definitions. Also add an explicit !UUID_ZERO guard.

findings.title: /api/presence/departure upserts user_presence WITHOUT setting last_active_at — overwrites a real activity timestamp with a stale beacon ts
findings.scenario: Two tabs of the same user. Tab A is active (heartbeat updates last_active_at every 60s via touch_my_presence(true)). Tab B closes — pagehide fires, beacon hits this endpoint. The upsert COMPLETELY replaces the row except for what's specified. Because last_active_at is NOT in the upsert payload, on conflict it's left intact — that's correct for THIS endpoint. BUT: the upsert also does not set last_active_at on INSERT (first-ever presence row). The DB column has NOT NULL default now(), so INSERT works. The real bug is subtler: the row's `last_seen_at` is overwritten to the beacon's `nowIso`, but the beacon ts on the body is the CLIENT's `now()` from when the page started dying, which may be slightly OLDER than the server's now(). Compared with touch_my_presence's `now()` in SQL, the beacon can REDUCE last_seen_at — but the upsert syntax used here does not include a GREATEST() guard, unlike sync_presence_from_audit() at migration 0024 line 128.
findings.blast_radius: Presence display flickers between 'active 30s ago' and 'active 2m ago' as the heartbeat + beacon race. Forensic timelines (audit_last vs presence_last_seen) skew. Memory note already flags 5-min beacon cooldown as the band-aid; this finding is the underlying lack of GREATEST() in the upsert.
findings.repro: Open two tabs. Pause the JS thread on Tab A for 90s, then unpause briefly. Close Tab B. The 5-min cooldown helps but cannot fully prevent the beacon from writing a slightly older last_seen_at because navigator.sendBeacon serializes payload at request time, and Node's fast response means the receive-time can be behind the heartbeat tick.
findings.fix:
Either use an explicit RPC (mirroring touch_my_presence but service-role-bypassed), or write raw SQL via admin.rpc with GREATEST:
```sql
INSERT INTO user_presence (user_id, last_seen_at, updated_at) VALUES ($1, now(), now())
ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO UPDATE SET
  last_seen_at = GREATEST(user_presence.last_seen_at, EXCLUDED.last_seen_at),
  updated_at = now();
```
Fastest patch: don't set updated_at from client-provided ts at all; always use server-side now() via an RPC.

findings.title: Realtime channel filter uses ONLY workspace_id — no actor exclusion. Card creator's own INSERT echo race vs. recentMutations 2s timeout
findings.scenario: Line 387 inside the realtime INSERT handler still checks `recentMutations.current.has("create")` — the literal string 'create'. But createCard at line 699 now marks markMutation(tempId) (timestamp ms string) and markMutation(data.id) (real UUID). The string 'create' is NEVER added to recentMutations. The has('create') branch is dead code — it always returns false. The subsequent `prev.some((c) => c.id === newCard.id)` IS what actually dedups (since data.id is patched into the temp card before the realtime echo arrives). But on slow connections the realtime echo can win the race vs. the INSERT response, inserting a duplicate card. The 2s timeout on markMutation(data.id) is too short if Supabase realtime fanout is delayed.
findings.blast_radius: Duplicate cards appear briefly under network stress; relies on the fallback `prev.some()` check to filter. The fallback works for the same-tab case but breaks for cross-tab: if user has 2 tabs open on the same workspace, Tab A creates a card, Tab A's tempId is patched to data.id BEFORE Tab B receives the realtime event. Tab B has no recentMutations.has(data.id) and adds the card cleanly. OK. But: if Tab B is also mid-creation, the prev.some check still works because UUIDs differ. The real flaw is the dead 'create' check (cosmetic) AND the 2s deletion window which can race the realtime echo.
findings.repro: Throttle Network in DevTools to Slow 3G. Create a card. The realtime INSERT echo may arrive 3+ seconds later, after markMutation(data.id)'s 2s timer cleared. Card briefly duplicates until React reconciliation.
findings.fix: 1) Remove the dead `recentMutations.current.has("create")` check at line 387. 2) Increase the dedup window for INSERT echoes specifically to 10s. 3) Better: switch the dedup to a Map<id, timestamp> and prune lazily (on each new event).

findings.title: team_members INSERT in inviteMember() omits joined_at + relies on client-tempid → realtime broadcast can deliver the row before tempId patch lands, splitting display
findings.scenario: Admin invites a new member from the UI. setMembers prepends a card with id=Date.now() (tempId). The .insert(...).select().single() fires. If RLS rejects (e.g. the admin's workspace_members.role is not in 0018's array['superadmin','admin','owner']), there is NO error handler — the temp row sits in state forever. Refresh removes it but the user doesn't know what happened. Additionally, when the insert succeeds, there is no markMutation guard: the team realtime INSERT handler at line 142 immediately adds the same row with the real UUID — now TWO rows exist (tempId + real UUID) until the .then() resolves and patches tempId → realId, at which point setMembers map() does in-place id swap. If the realtime handler beats the .then(), the temp row stays forever.
findings.blast_radius: Ghost pending team rows that persist until refresh. RLS rejection produces silent data divergence with no toast. Iron-law spirit: a user invite that 'succeeded' in the UI was actually rejected by the DB.
findings.repro: 1) Patch test user's workspace_members.role = 'viewer'. 2) From UI, invite a new member. 3) The pending row appears in the list. 4) Refresh — gone. No toast.
findings.fix: Add error handling + temp-row rollback like pipeline-context.tsx createCard. Also add markMutation(tempId) + dedup in the realtime handler at line 142.

findings.title: auto-revise webhook race: enqueue happens AFTER moveCard's notes UPDATE — concurrent edits between webhook arrival and enqueue lose the kickback note
findings.scenario: Reviewer A clicks Kickback with note 'fix the hook'. submitKickback writes posts.notes='A (...): Fix submitted — fix the hook' + stage='revision_needed'. Supabase webhook fires → extractLatestReviewerNote returns 'fix the hook' → enqueues ai_generation_jobs row. Meanwhile Reviewer B also clicks Kickback at nearly the same instant. Their submitKickback APPENDS another note. The first webhook is mid-flight when B's update arrives. The DB triggers a SECOND webhook event. But the in-flight check at line 103-109 finds the queued job from A and returns 204. B's note is silently dropped — the AI revises against A's note only.
findings.blast_radius: Reviewer B's kickback request is silently ignored. Posts.notes still has B's text appended (visible in the drawer's history), but the AI never sees it. The card moves to revision_needed → AI revises against A's text → flips back to awaiting_approval → B looks at the AI revision and it doesn't address their note → they kickback again. Loops waste OpenAI cost.
findings.repro: Two reviewers open the same Awaiting Approval card. Both click Kickback within 5s of each other. The second kickback's note is dropped.
findings.fix: Either (a) inside the in-flight check, append B's note to the existing job's payload via UPDATE instead of returning 204; or (b) drop the in-flight check entirely and let the worker dedupe on plan_row_id (already does at worker.ts line 294). (b) is cleaner.

findings.title: 0007_rls_v2 posts_insert_v2 array['superadmin','admin','owner','approver','creative_director','editor','social_media_specialist','video_editor','graphic_designer','specialist'] OMITS 'technician' but content_plan_rows policy (0022) INCLUDES it — schema drift, technician can plan but cannot create posts
findings.evidence:
create policy "posts_insert_v2" on posts for insert
  with check (is_active_workspace_member(
    workspace_id,
    array['superadmin','admin','owner','approver','creative_director','editor','social_media_specialist','video_editor','graphic_designer','specialist']
  ));
-- vs. 0022 plan_rows_write_writers:
--   array['superadmin','admin','owner','approver','creative_director','editor','social_media_specialist','video_editor','graphic_designer','specialist','technician']
findings.scenario: A 'technician' user (one of the user_role enum values from migration 0000) can write to content_plan_rows (their AI plan grid) but the resulting AI worker.ts insertGeneratedPost runs as service_role so the insert succeeds. However if a technician directly creates a card via the UI's CreatePostModal, their .insert() on posts is RLS-rejected. The role enum from 0000 includes 'technician' and 'specialist' as legitimate values; the RLS arrays are inconsistent.
findings.blast_radius: technician-role users can plan AI posts but cannot create manual posts. No toast (per DATA-001), so they think the card was created and lose it on refresh.
findings.repro: 1) Update a test user's workspace_members.role='technician'. 2) Click Create Post in the UI, fill it out, submit. 3) Modal closes, card appears. 4) Refresh — card is gone.
findings.fix: Add 'technician' to posts_insert_v2 and posts_update_v2 array in a new migration 0027. Verify the same list across media_assets and brand_playbook policies — currently brand_playbook is admin-only which is probably intentional but should be documented.

findings.title: workspace_members INSERT in provision route lacks ON CONFLICT — racing parallel provision calls (same user, two tabs, fast login) can violate (user_id, workspace_id) uniqueness if a UNIQUE index exists, or create duplicates if it doesn't
findings.scenario: User opens two tabs simultaneously on first-ever login. Tab A hits /api/workspace/provision → SELECT returns no existing row → starts INSERT. Tab B hits same endpoint at the same instant → SELECT returns no existing row → starts INSERT. Both INSERTs race. If migration 0002 has a UNIQUE (user_id, workspace_id) index on workspace_members, one INSERT errors with 23505. The response to that tab is 500 (no try/catch on the insert at line 54). User sees 'workspace not configured' or undefined behavior.
findings.blast_radius: Authenticated user gets a 500 on first login in 2-tab scenario. Workspace provisioning is the foundation of every other RLS check, so failure here cascades into 'cannot read posts' for the affected tab until refresh. Iron-law violation in spirit — posts disappear from the affected tab.
findings.repro: Hard to repro without simulating exact tab-open simultaneity. Use a load test with 2 parallel GETs to /api/workspace/provision with the same Bearer token for a never-provisioned user.
findings.fix: Change to upsert: `await admin.from("workspace_members").upsert({...}, { onConflict: 'user_id,workspace_id', ignoreDuplicates: true });`. Verify the unique index exists — search 0002_tenant_model.sql for the constraint. If no unique index, ADD ONE in a follow-up migration plus use upsert.

findings.title: 0026 lockdown rejects manual stage='posted' transitions BUT allows direct INSERTs of stage='posted' posts without posted_at — orphan posted rows could appear via backfill scripts
findings.scenario: The 0026 comment at lines 95-97 explicitly says 'INSERT path is unchanged — a post being created directly in posted (e.g. a back-fill) is allowed; the trigger only guards transitions.' This is a documented intentional gap. However, in practice the gap means any future restore script, n8n bulk-import, or admin SQL paste that inserts stage='posted' rows is NOT required to set posted_at. The PostsRow type in pipeline-context.tsx maps posted_at → postedAt → the UI badge. A direct insert without posted_at would render 'Live' badge with broken tooltip and a fallback icon without href.
findings.blast_radius: UI rendering glitch only (the 0015 protect-trigger still blocks deletion of those posted rows once they exist). No data loss. Documented intentional behavior, but the audit trail is incomplete.
findings.repro: INSERT INTO posts (id, title, stage, workspace_id, content_type) VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), 'Back-fill', 'posted', '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'image'); — succeeds, no posted_at. UI shows 'Live' with empty tooltip.
findings.fix: Either accept the documented behavior (low priority) or add a CHECK constraint that's enforced on INSERT as well as UPDATE: `ALTER TABLE posts ADD CONSTRAINT posts_posted_requires_posted_at CHECK (stage <> 'posted' OR posted_at IS NOT NULL);`. The CHECK fires on both INSERT and UPDATE without needing trigger logic.
